name: The Azud Route (PR-CV 456) — Circular along the Tuéjar River
name_original: La Ruta del Azud (PR-CV 456) — Circular por el Río Tuéjar
region_intro: Hiking around Tuéjar offers diverse landscapes within the Los Serranos county of Valencia. The region is characterized by the Tuéjar River, which carves through varied terrain including natural pools and waterfalls. Hikers can explore river valleys, ascend to viewpoints, and traverse paths leading through historical sites. The area provides a blend of natural features and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Tuéjar The most popular hiking route is The Azud Route (PR-CV 456) — Circular along the Tuéjar River, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Tuéjar River, passing by its source and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Waterfall loop from Casas de Zagra, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This route features waterfalls and varied terrain, offering a scenic loop. Local hikers also love the Azud de Tuéjar – Tuéjar Ice House loop from Tuéjar, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through the Azud area and past the historic Ice House, often completed in about 3 hours. Hiking around Tuéjar is defined by river walks, ascents to viewpoints, and paths leading to historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to moderate routes. The routes in Tuéjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Tuéjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tuéjar?
A: Tuéjar offers a variety of hiking trails, with over 10 routes available on komoot. These include 4 easy routes and 7 moderate routes, catering to different fitness levels and interests.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes around Tuéjar?
A: Hiking around Tuéjar offers diverse terrain. You can expect paths along the Tuéjar River with loose rocks, dirt trails, gravel roads, and occasional water crossings. Some routes also involve ascents to viewpoints on gravel roads and smaller trails of shale rocks, providing varied scenery from river walks to historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Tuéjar?
A: Yes, Tuéjar has 4 easy hiking routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Azud de Tuéjar loop from Tuéjar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 miles (11.6 km) long and takes around 2 hours to complete, with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Tuéjar?
A: Yes, Tuéjar offers several circular hiking routes. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822566222/the-azud-route-pr-cv-456-circular-along-the-tuejar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Azud Route (PR-CV 456) — Circular along the Tuéjar River&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that follows the Tuéjar River, passing by its source and natural pools.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools to see on the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its beautiful water features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Tuéjar&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque recreational area where the Tuéjar river is born, forming a lagoon known as "El Pozo de las Escaleras" ideal for bathing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorros de Barchel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are also spectacular, especially after rain, with falls up to 50 meters.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites. The Corrales de Silla Caves, a UNESCO World Heritage site, house Neolithic cave paintings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Tuéjar&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant natural landmark. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Tuéjar – Tuéjar Ice House loop from Tuéjar&lt;/a&gt; will lead you past a historic Ice House.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tuéjar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the refreshing river walks, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites. Many appreciate the well-maintained trails and the opportunity for wild swimming in spots like El Azud.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls specifically?
A: Yes, if you're looking for waterfalls, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall loop from Casas de Zagra&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path features waterfalls and varied terrain, offering a scenic loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall loop from Cascada de Bercolón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Tuéjar accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to trailheads can vary, Tuéjar itself is generally accessible by bus from larger towns in the Valencia region. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most current information on reaching the village, from where many trails begin.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tuéjar?
A: Tuéjar is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring, especially after rains, is particularly beautiful as the river has a higher water volume, making the Nacimiento del Río Tuéjar route vibrant. The refreshing 18°C water at El Azud makes warmer months ideal for combining hiking with a swim. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and colorful scenery.

Q: Are there options for longer or more challenging hikes in Tuéjar?
A: While Tuéjar primarily offers easy to moderate routes, some trails can be combined or extended for a longer experience. The moderate routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822566222/the-azud-route-pr-cv-456-circular-along-the-tuejar-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Azud Route (PR-CV 456)&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours to complete, providing a good workout with significant elevation changes for those seeking a more extended adventure.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Tuéjar?
A: Many trails in Tuéjar are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in specific recreational zones like El Azud.
tips: Within the Natural Park of the Rio Tuéjar, in the Lácaba area at an altitude of 635 meters, we find a small treasure from the pre-industrial era; an old snowfield that was restored in 2008.
Viewpoint with very nice views towards the town of Tuéjar and the Natural Park of the Birth of the Tuéjar River. You can also see the recreational area of the Azud.

This area gets its name from the old dam that supplied the town's main ditch. Today it is a very beautiful and picturesque recreational area with lush vegetation and pure, crystal clear water for bathing.
It is at the same time the place where the Tuéjar river is born, which forms a lagoon known as “El Pozo de las Escaleras” whose waters are at 18°C all year round, which makes it an ideal area for bathing in the summer months. .
